title: Compare secrets in constant time
description: Use a constant-time comparison for MACs, tokens, and other secrets so an attacker cannot recover them via timing.
body: Use when comparing a user-supplied value against a secret (HMAC tags, API tokens, password-reset codes). Do not use a normal equality operator that returns early on the first differing byte, and avoid logging or branching on partial matches. Actions: use the library's constant-time comparison (crypto.timingSafeEqual, sodium_memcmp, hmac.compare_digest) over the full fixed-length values, and verify lengths in a way that does not itself leak. Validation signals include a test confirming the comparison path is constant-time and a code review that no short-circuit compare touches a secret. Risk boundary: a timing side channel can leak a token byte-by-byte across many requests; treat early-exit comparison of secrets as a security defect.
